words.definition_en: to move faster or to make something move faster
words.example: The car accelerated as it overtook me.
words.example_vi: Chiếc xe tăng tốc khi nó vượt qua tôi.

words.definition_en: to move closer to someone or something; a way of considering or doing something
words.definition_vi: tiếp cận, lại gần; cách tiếp cận
words.example: As we approached the city, the traffic got heavier.
words.example_vi: Khi chúng tôi đến gần thành phố, giao thông trở nên đông đúc hơn.

words.definition_en: to move up or to go up something
words.example: The path ascends steeply up the hill.
words.example_vi: Con đường dốc ngược lên đồi.

words.definition_en: to move up or away after hitting a surface
words.example: The ball bounced off the wall.
words.example_vi: Quả bóng nảy ra từ bức tường.

words.definition_en: to climb up, across, or into somewhere with difficulty, using your hands and feet
words.definition_vi: leo trèo, bò (một cách vất vả)
words.example: They clambered over the rocks.
words.example_vi: Họ leo qua những tảng đá.

words.definition_en: to close or hold something very tightly, often because of anger or pain
words.definition_vi: mím chặt, nắm chặt (răng, nắm đấm)
words.example: He clenched his fists in frustration.
words.example_vi: Anh ta nắm chặt tay vì bực bội.

words.definition_en: to hold something tightly, usually because you are afraid or in pain
words.definition_vi: giật lấy, nắm chặt (vì sợ hoặc đau)
words.example: She clutched her handbag tightly as she walked through the crowd.
words.example_vi: Cô ấy ôm chặt túi xách khi đi qua đám đông.

words.definition_en: to move on your hands and knees
words.example: The baby is just starting to crawl.
words.example_vi: Đứa bé vừa mới bắt đầu biết bò.

words.definition_en: to move slowly, quietly, and carefully, usually in order to avoid being noticed
words.example: I saw someone creeping around the house.
words.example_vi: Tôi thấy ai đó đang rón rén quanh nhà.

words.definition_en: to go somewhere very quickly
words.example: I must dash or I'll be late for work.
words.example_vi: Tôi phải đi ngay kẻo muộn làm.

words.definition_en: to go down
words.example: The sun gradually descended below the horizon.
words.example_vi: Mặt trời dần dần lặn xuống dưới chân trời.

words.definition_en: to be pushed along by moving water or air
words.example: The boat drifted out to sea.
words.example_vi: Con thuyền trôi dạt ra khơi.

words.definition_en: to leave your own country and go to live permanently in another country
words.example: His family emigrated from Italy to Australia in the 1950s.
words.example_vi: Gia đình anh ấy đã di cư từ Ý sang Úc vào những năm 1950.

words.definition_en: to stay on the surface of a liquid and not sink
words.example: Leaves floated on the surface of the pond.
words.example_vi: Những chiếc lá nổi trên mặt ao.

words.definition_en: the continuous movement of a liquid in one direction
words.example: The river flows through the city center.
words.example_vi: Con sông chảy qua trung tâm thành phố.

words.definition_en: to use your hands with difficulty to try to get hold of something or find something
words.example: She fumbled in her bag for her keys.
words.example_vi: Cô ấy lúng túng lục lọi trong túi để tìm chìa khóa.

words.definition_en: to move your hand, arm, or head to express an idea or feeling
words.definition_vi: điệu bộ, cử chỉ, ra hiệu
words.example: He gestured towards the door, indicating that we should leave.
words.example_vi: Anh ấy ra hiệu về phía cửa, ám chỉ rằng chúng tôi nên rời đi.

words.definition_en: to move smoothly and easily, as if without effort
words.example: The skaters glided across the ice.
words.example_vi: Những người trượt băng lướt đi trên mặt băng.

words.definition_en: to take hold of something or someone suddenly and roughly
words.example: I grabbed my coat and ran out.
words.example_vi: Tôi vớ lấy chiếc áo khoác và chạy ra ngoài.

words.definition_en: to take hold of something or someone firmly with your hands
words.example: He grasped my hand and shook it firmly.
words.example_vi: Tôi đã mất khá nhiều thời gian mới nắm bắt được mục tiêu của trò chơi này là gì.

words.definition_en: to move by jumping on one foot
words.example: I had to hop because I'd hurt my foot.
words.example_vi: Tôi phải nhảy lò cò vì chân bị đau.

words.definition_en: someone who comes to live in a different country
words.example: The government is trying to restrict the number of illegal immigrants.
words.example_vi: Chính phủ đang cố gắng hạn chế số lượng người nhập cư bất hợp pháp.

words.definition_en: to run at a slow, steady speed, especially for exercise
words.example: I go jogging every morning.
words.example_vi: Tôi đi chạy bộ mỗi sáng.

words.definition_en: to jump high into the air or over something
words.example: The salmon were leaping out of the water.
words.example_vi: Những con cá hồi đang nhảy vọt lên khỏi mặt nước.

words.definition_en: to walk with regular steps of equal length, like a soldier
words.example: The soldiers marched past the palace.
words.example_vi: Những người lính diễu hành qua cung điện.

words.definition_en: if birds or animals migrate, they travel from one part of the world to another at the same time each year
words.definition_vi: di cư (động vật, hoặc theo mùa)
words.example: These birds migrate south for the winter.
words.example_vi: Những loài chim này di cư về phương nam để tránh rét.

words.definition_en: to show where something is by holding out your finger or a long thin object
words.example: She pointed to the map on the wall.
words.example_vi: Cô ấy chỉ vào tấm bản đồ trên tường.

words.definition_en: to hit someone or something with your fist
words.example: He punched the wall in anger.
words.example_vi: Anh ta đấm vào tường trong cơn giận dữ.

words.definition_en: someone who has been forced to leave their country for help and protection in another country
words.example: Thousands of refugees are fleeing the war.
words.example_vi: Hàng ngàn người tị nạn đang chạy trốn khỏi chiến tranh.

words.definition_en: to move or travel around an area without any definite aim or direction
words.example: The sheep are allowed to roam freely on the hills.
words.example_vi: Những con cừu được phép đi lang thang tự do trên đồi.

words.definition_en: to move by turning over and over
words.example: The ball rolled down the hill.
words.example_vi: Quả bóng lăn xuống đồi.

words.definition_en: to move or turn in a circle around a central point
words.example: The Earth rotates once every 24 hours.
words.example_vi: Trái đất tự quay quanh trục một lần mỗi 24 giờ.

words.definition_en: the way from one place to another
words.example: Which is the quickest route to the airport?
words.example_vi: Đường nào đi đến sân bay nhanh nhất?

words.definition_en: to go down below the surface of water, mud, etc.
words.example: The Titanic sank after hitting an iceberg.
words.example_vi: Tàu Titanic đã bị chìm sau khi va phải một tảng băng trôi.

words.definition_en: to slide across the ground in an uncontrolled way
words.example: The car skidded on the icy road.
words.example_vi: Chiếc xe bị trượt trên con đường đóng băng.

words.definition_en: to move forwards by jumping first on one foot and then on the other
words.example: The little girl skipped happily along the path.
words.example_vi: Cô bé tung tăng nhảy chân sáo dọc theo con đường.

words.definition_en: to move smoothly over a surface
words.example: The kids were sliding on the ice.
words.example_vi: Lũ trẻ đang trượt trên băng.

words.definition_en: to slide a short distance by accident so that you fall or nearly fall
words.example: I slipped on a banana skin.
words.example_vi: Tôi bị trượt chân vì một vỏ chuối.

words.definition_en: to move by lifting your foot and putting it down in a different place
words.example: She took a step towards him.
words.example_vi: Cô ấy tiến một bước về phía anh ta.

words.definition_en: to walk with long steps
words.example: He strode across the room to greet her.
words.example_vi: Anh ấy sải bước qua phòng để chào cô ấy.

words.definition_en: to hit your foot on something and fall or nearly fall
words.example: I tripped over the cat and fell.
words.example_vi: Tôi vấp phải con mèo và bị ngã.

words.definition_en: the speed of something in a particular direction
words.example: The missile reached a velocity of 3,000 miles per hour.
words.example_vi: Tên lửa đạt vận tốc 3.000 dặm một giờ.

words.definition_en: to travel from place to place without a particular direction or purpose
words.example: We spent the morning wandering around the old town.
words.example_vi: Chúng tôi dành cả buổi sáng để lang thang quanh khu phố cổ.

words.definition_en: to move your hand from side to side in the air to say hello or goodbye
words.example: She waved goodbye as the train left the station.
words.example_vi: Cô ấy vẫy tay chào tạm biệt khi con tàu rời ga.

words.definition_en: a company that provides regular flights for passengers or goods
words.example: Which airline are you flying with?
words.example_vi: Bạn bay của hãng hàng không nào?

words.definition_en: the goods carried by a ship, aircraft, or other large vehicle
words.definition_vi: hàng hóa (vận chuyển bằng tàu/máy bay)
words.example: The ship was carrying a cargo of timber.
words.example_vi: Con tàu đang chở một lô hàng gỗ.

words.definition_en: one of the vehicles that are joined together to make a train
words.example: The front carriage of the train was damaged in the accident.
words.example_vi: Toa tàu phía trước đã bị hư hỏng trong vụ tai nạn.

words.definition_en: to hire a boat, plane, or bus for a particular use
words.example: They chartered a boat to go fishing.
words.example_vi: Họ đã thuê riêng một chiếc thuyền để đi câu cá.

words.definition_en: to travel regularly to and from work
words.definition_vi: đi làm đều đặn hàng ngày
words.example: He commutes to London every day.
words.example_vi: Anh ấy đi làm hằng ngày đến London.

words.definition_en: the place where someone or something is going
words.example: We reached our destination after a long journey.
words.example_vi: Chúng tôi đã đến đích sau một cuộc hành trình dài.

words.definition_en: someone who walks long distances in the mountains or country for pleasure
words.example: The hills are popular with hikers.
words.example_vi: Những ngọn đồi này rất phổ biến với những người đi bộ đường dài.

words.definition_en: someone who travels by asking other people to take them in their car
words.example: I used to be a hitchhiker when I was a student.
words.example_vi: Tôi từng là một người đi nhờ xe khi còn là sinh viên.

words.definition_en: the feeling of being very tired and sometimes confused after a long flight
words.definition_vi: mệt mỏi sau chuyến bay dài (lệch múi giờ)
words.example: I'm still suffering from jet lag.
words.example_vi: Tôi vẫn còn đang bị mệt mỏi do lệch múi giờ.

words.definition_en: the amount of space available for your legs when you are sitting in a car, plane, etc.
words.example: There's not much legroom in the back of this car.
words.example_vi: Không có nhiều chỗ để chân ở phía sau chiếc xe này.

words.definition_en: to put a large quantity of things into a vehicle or container
words.example: The truck was carrying a heavy load.
words.example_vi: Chiếc xe tải đang chở một tải trọng nặng.

words.definition_en: someone who is walking past a place by chance
words.example: The accident was witnessed by several passersby.
words.example_vi: Vụ tai nạn đã được chứng kiến bởi vài người qua đường.

words.definition_en: someone who is walking, especially in a town or city
words.example: This area is for pedestrians only.
words.example_vi: Khu vực này chỉ dành cho người đi bộ.

words.definition_en: a structure built out from the land over water
words.example: We walked along the pier to see the boats.
words.example_vi: Chúng tôi đi dọc theo cầu tàu để ngắm những con thuyền.

words.definition_en: someone who flies an aircraft; to fly an aircraft
words.definition_vi: phi công; điều khiển (máy bay, tàu)
words.example: He's a pilot for a major airline.
words.example_vi: Anh ấy là phi công cho một hãng hàng không lớn.

words.definition_en: a hard surface next to a sea or river, where boats can stop
words.example: A crowd gathered on the quay to see the ship arrive.
words.example_vi: Một đám đông tụ tập trên bến cảng để xem con tàu cập bến.

words.definition_en: the price of a journey to a place and back again
words.example: The return fare is much cheaper than two singles.
words.example_vi: Giá vé khứ hồi rẻ hơn nhiều so với hai vé một chiều.

words.definition_en: a journey to a place and back again
words.example: The round trip takes about four hours.
words.example_vi: Chuyến đi khứ hồi mất khoảng bốn giờ.

words.definition_en: to control the direction in which a vehicle moves
words.example: He steered the car through the narrow streets.
words.example_vi: Anh ấy lái xe qua những con phố hẹp.

words.definition_en: a man whose job is to look after the passengers on a plane, train, or ship
words.example: The steward brought us some drinks.
words.example_vi: Nam tiếp viên đã mang cho chúng tôi một ít đồ uống.

words.definition_en: stop people from entering an area by putting something such as a rope around it
words.definition_vi: phong tỏa bằng rào chắn/dây thừng
words.example: The police cordoned off the area around the suspicious package.
words.example_vi: Cảnh sát đã phong tỏa khu vực xung quanh gói hàng khả nghi.

words.definition_en: move towards someone quietly and slowly, especially because you want to surprise them
words.definition_vi: rón rén lại gần (để gây bất ngờ)
words.example: Don't creep up on me like that!
words.example_vi: Đừng có lẻn đến sau lưng tôi như vậy!

words.definition_en: move more slowly than other people so that you are behind them; make less progress or be less successful than other people
words.definition_vi: tụt lại phía sau; chậm trễ tiến độ
words.example: He fell behind in his schoolwork after being ill.
words.example_vi: Anh ấy bị tụt lại phía sau trong việc học ở trường sau khi bị ốm.

words.definition_en: become lost or go to the wrong place
words.example: The letter must have gone astray in the post.
words.example_vi: Bức thư chắc hẳn đã bị thất lạc trong lúc gửi bưu điện.

words.definition_en: prevent someone from going somewhere by getting in front of them; prevent something from taking place
words.definition_vi: chặn đầu; ngăn chặn điều gì xảy ra
words.example: We'll head them off at the bridge.
words.example_vi: Chúng ta sẽ chặn đường bọn họ tại cây cầu.

words.definition_en: stop someone or something from moving forwards
words.example: Police struggled to hold back the crowd.
words.example_vi: Cảnh sát đã vất vả để kiềm chế đám đông.

words.definition_en: start living in a different house or flat (with)
words.example: She's moving in with her boyfriend.
words.example_vi: Cô ấy đang dọn đến sống chung với bạn trai.

words.definition_en: permanently leave the house or flat where you live
words.example: Our neighbors moved out last week.
words.example_vi: Hàng xóm của chúng tôi đã dọn đi vào tuần trước.

words.definition_en: change your position in order to make space for someone or something
words.definition_vi: né sang một bên, xê ra
words.example: Move over so I can sit down.
words.example_vi: Nhích qua một chút để tôi có thể ngồi xuống.

words.definition_en: stop by the side of the road in a car or other vehicle
words.example: The police pulled him over for speeding.
words.example_vi: Cảnh sát đã yêu cầu anh ta tấp xe vào lề vì chạy quá tốc độ.

words.definition_en: leave secretly
words.example: We slipped away before the party ended.
words.example_vi: Chúng tôi đã lẻn ra ngoài trước khi bữa tiệc kết thúc.

words.definition_en: move so that sb can pass you; leave a job so that sb else can take over
words.definition_vi: bước sang bên; nhường chỗ/nhường chức
words.example: It's time for the director to step aside and let a younger person take over.
words.example_vi: Đã đến lúc giám đốc phải lùi bước và để một người trẻ hơn tiếp quản.

words.definition_en: visit somewhere before continuing to another place
words.definition_vi: ghé thăm, dừng lại giữa chặng
words.example: We stopped off in Paris on our way to Rome.
words.example_vi: Chúng tôi đã dừng chân ở Paris trên đường đến Rome.

words.definition_en: (of an object) have one end move upwards; turn a container upside down
words.example: The bench tipped up when I sat on the end.
words.example_vi: Chiếc ghế dài bị bênh lên khi tôi ngồi vào phía đầu ghế.

words.definition_en: leave one place and travel to another; stop discussing sth and begin discussing sth different
words.definition_vi: tiếp tục đi; chuyển sang chủ đề khác
words.example: Let's move on to the next item on the agenda.
words.example_vi: Hãy chuyển sang mục tiếp theo trong chương trình nghị sự.

words.definition_en: leave a meeting, performance, etc before the end; suddenly leave a relationship, family, job, etc
words.definition_vi: bỏ cuộc giữa chừng; rời bỏ (gia đình, công việc)
words.example: He walked out on his wife and children.
words.example_vi: Anh ta đã bỏ rơi vợ con mình.

words.definition_en: very close (to)
words.example: The hotel is just a stone's throw from the beach.
words.example_vi: Khách sạn chỉ cách bãi biển một quãng ngắn.

words.definition_en: in a straight line (used for measuring distance)
words.example: It's five miles as the crow flies, but about seven by road.
words.example_vi: Đường chim bay là 5 dặm, nhưng đường bộ thì khoảng 7 dặm.

words.definition_en: go straight forward; do what you feel is right in a situation
words.definition_vi: cứ đi thẳng; làm theo trực giác
words.example: Just follow your nose and you'll find the station.
words.example_vi: Cứ đi thẳng là bạn sẽ tìm thấy nhà ga thôi.

words.definition_en: a long way from any town or city
words.definition_vi: ở nơi hẻo lánh, đồng không mông quạnh
words.example: The car broke down in the middle of nowhere.
words.example_vi: Chiếc xe bị hỏng ở một nơi đồng không mông quạnh.

words.definition_en: become confused about where you are
words.example: I lost my bearings in the dark and couldn't find the way back.
words.example_vi: Tôi bị mất phương hướng trong bóng tối và không tìm được đường về.

words.definition_en: go towards someone or something in the quickest and most direct way
words.example: As soon as he arrived, he made a beeline for the food.
words.example_vi: Ngay khi vừa đến, anh ta đã lao ngay tới chỗ đồ ăn.

words.definition_en: away from populated areas or areas popular with tourists
words.definition_vi: nơi hẻo lánh, ít người lui tới
words.example: We found a tiny hotel off the beaten track.
words.example_vi: Chúng tôi đã tìm thấy một khách sạn nhỏ nằm ở nơi hẻo lánh.

words.definition_en: suddenly stop, for example because you are surprised
words.definition_vi: dừng sững lại (vì ngạc nhiên, sợ hãi)
words.example: I stopped dead in my tracks when I saw the snake.
words.example_vi: Tôi đứng khựng lại khi nhìn thấy con rắn.

words.definition_en: follow a path that is quicker and shorter than the usual way
words.example: If we take a short cut through the park, we'll be there in five minutes.
words.example_vi: Nếu chúng ta đi đường tắt xuyên qua công viên, chúng ta sẽ đến đó trong năm phút.

words.definition_en: follow a longer path than the usual one because it is more attractive
words.definition_vi: đi đường vòng để ngắm cảnh
words.example: We decided to take the scenic route home.
words.example_vi: Chúng tôi quyết định đi đường vòng để ngắm cảnh khi về nhà.

exercises.label: A
exercises.instruction: Complete using the correct form of the words in the boxes.

items.sentence: I ___ my school bag from the table and rushed out of the house.
items.explanation: 'Grab' means to take hold of something suddenly and roughly.

items.sentence: Grace ___ in her bag for her car keys and finally found them.
items.explanation: 'Fumble' means to use your hands with difficulty to try to find something.

items.sentence: A man ran from the burning house, ___ a baby in his arms.
items.explanation: 'Clutching' describes holding something tightly, often in a desperate situation.

items.sentence: The old man ___ his finger straight at me and I felt a chill go down my spine.
items.explanation: 'Point' is to show where something is with a finger.

items.sentence: As the train left the station, Charlie ___ goodbye.
items.explanation: 'Wave' is the motion of the hand to say goodbye.

items.sentence: Jamie came home from school with a black eye because someone had ___ him.
items.explanation: 'Punch' means to hit someone with a fist.

items.sentence: I asked Karen where the phone was and without taking her eyes off the TV she ___ lazily in the direction of the kitchen.
items.explanation: 'Gesture' is to move a hand to express or indicate something.

items.sentence: Lina ___ her fists tightly in anger but managed to control herself.
items.explanation: 'Clenched' is used specifically for fists or teeth being held tightly.

items.sentence: He ___ my hand firmly and I could see that he was trying to thank me.
items.explanation: 'Grasp' means to take hold of something firmly.

items.sentence: The burglar ___ very quietly along the corridor, trying not to wake anybody.
items.explanation: 'Crept' (past of creep) means to move quietly and slowly to avoid being noticed.

items.sentence: How long do you think you could ___ on one leg?
items.explanation: 'Hop' means to jump on one foot.

items.sentence: I ___ in the park most mornings because I like to keep myself in shape.
items.explanation: 'Jog' is a slow, steady run for exercise.

items.sentence: Just ___ this way, sir, and the customer services manager will be with you in a moment.
items.explanation: 'Step' is used here to mean walk a short distance.

items.sentence: When we heard the noise, we quickly ___ round the corner to see what had happened.
items.explanation: 'Dashed' means to move very quickly.

items.sentence: Before he learnt to walk, my younger brother used to ___ everywhere on all fours.
items.explanation: 'Crawl' is the movement of babies on hands and knees.

items.sentence: The army ___ for two days before they came to the outskirts of the city.
items.explanation: 'Marched' is the regular, rhythmic walking of soldiers.

items.sentence: The speaker ___ onto the stage and smiled confidently at the audience.
items.explanation: 'Strode' (past of stride) means to walk with long, confident steps.

items.sentence: When he saw me coming, our dog ___ over the gate and came to greet me.
items.explanation: 'Leapt' (past of leap) means to jump a large distance/height.

items.sentence: Polly was so happy she ___ along the pavement to school, jumping from one foot to the other.
items.explanation: 'Skipped' is a rhythmic jump-walk expressing happiness.

items.sentence: When the men were finally found, they had been ___ at sea in a tiny boat for days.
items.explanation: 'Drifting' means being moved by water without control.

items.sentence: Wolves used to ___ this whole area, looking for food.
items.explanation: 'Roam' means to wander over a large area.

items.sentence: I was bored so I ___ into the centre of town and did a bit of window shopping.
items.explanation: 'Wandered' means to walk without a specific destination.

items.sentence: I didn't see the oil on the floor and I ___ on it.
items.explanation: 'Slipped' is an accidental slide, usually leading to a fall.

items.sentence: This wardrobe is really heavy, but maybe we can ___ it along the ground.
items.explanation: 'Slide' is to move something smoothly along a surface.

items.sentence: I didn't see the step and I ___ over it.
items.explanation: 'Tripped' means to catch one's foot and fall.

items.sentence: The road was wet, so when I pressed the brakes the car ___.
items.explanation: 'Skidded' refers to a vehicle's wheels sliding uncontrollably.

exercises.label: B
exercises.instruction: Complete using the words in bold in the correct form.

items.sentence: I ___ over a rock and looked down into the valley below us. We had ___ to 6,000 metres above sea level and the air was getting thin. Nick was injured, and I knew we would have to ___ soon to get medical treatment.
items.explanation: 'Clambered' (to climb with difficulty), 'ascended' (to go up), 'descend' (to go down).

items.sentence: The pilot held the controls. The engines were dead and the plane was ___ towards the airfield. She knew that if she didn't have the ___ to reach the runway, there was no way she could ___ . She just hoped that the air would continue to ___ over the wings and stop her from ___ too low. She ___ the runway with her heart in her throat.
items.answer: gliding, velocity, accelerate, flow, sinking, approached
items.explanation: 'Gliding' (flying without engine), 'velocity' (speed), 'accelerate' (speed up), 'flow' (air movement), 'sinking' (going down), 'approached' (got closer).

items.sentence: The arctic tern is an incredible bird. It ___ thousands of miles each year, stopping occasionally to ___ on the ocean to rest. How it remembers the ___ is still not fully understood.
items.explanation: 'Migrates' (seasonal travel), 'float' (rest on water), 'route' (the path taken).

items.sentence: In basketball, you must ___ the ball when you are moving with it. When you take a shot, you should let the ball ___ off your fingers so that it ___ in the air. That makes it more accurate.
items.explanation: 'Bounce' (dribbling), 'roll' (moving off fingers), 'rotate' (spinning in the air).

items.sentence: In the 1950s, many people ___ from the West Indies to England. Life was often hard for these ___ , but they eventually became part of British life. Today, Britain also welcomes ___ from parts of the world that are affected by war.
items.explanation: 'Emigrated' (left country), 'immigrants' (people coming to live), 'refugees' (fleeing war).

exercises.label: C
exercises.instruction: Complete using the correct form of the words in the box.

items.sentence: People started to ___ to work, travelling home in a crowded train...
items.explanation: To travel regularly between home and work.

items.sentence: ...train ___ each day.
items.explanation: A section of a train.

items.sentence: Now a new ___ , Officeways, plans to extend the idea...
items.explanation: A company providing air travel.

items.sentence: ...who could afford to ___ a plane or helicopter...
items.explanation: To hire a vehicle for private use.

items.sentence: ...which travels to ___ throughout Europe...
items.explanation: Places where someone is going.

items.sentence: You might have less ___ than on the train...
items.explanation: Space for legs while sitting.

items.sentence: ...Officeways claims their ___ are specially trained...
items.explanation: Personnel serving passengers on a plane/train.

items.sentence: ...there's no risk of ___ !
items.explanation: Tiredness from flying across time zones.

exercises.label: D
exercises.instruction: Circle the correct word or phrase.

items.sentence: Running along the seafront, there was a short concrete (pier / quay), where a dozen fishing boats bobbed in the afternoon sun.
items.explanation: A quay is a stone or metal platform alongside water for loading/unloading boats.

items.sentence: Quite a few resorts in Britain have long (piers / quays), which stick out into the sea...
items.explanation: A pier is a structure leading out from the shore into a body of water.

items.sentence: This area is for (passersby / pedestrians) only and is closed to all traffic.
items.explanation: Pedestrians are people walking in a specific area (like a street).

items.sentence: After the accident, (passersby / pedestrians) described the driver...
items.explanation: Passersby are people who happen to be walking past a scene.

items.sentence: Keep both hands on the wheel and you'll find it easier to (pilot / steer) the car.
items.explanation: Steer is used for controlling land vehicles like cars.

items.sentence: I'm hoping to train so that I can (pilot / steer) commercial aircraft.
items.explanation: Pilot is the specific verb for controlling aircraft.

items.sentence: As I came out of the petrol station, I saw a (hiker / hitchhiker) with his thumb out...
items.explanation: A hitchhiker thumbs a lift from cars.

items.sentence: Local (hikers / hitchhikers) insist that they have the right to walk across the field...
items.explanation: Hikers walk long distances for pleasure/exercise.

items.sentence: It's three and a half hours each way, so the (return fare / round trip) takes seven hours.
items.explanation: Round trip refers to the whole journey there and back.

items.sentence: A single is €40, but the (return fare / round trip) is actually only €55.
items.explanation: Return fare refers to the price of the ticket.

items.sentence: A lorry lost its (cargo / load) on the motorway and there's white paint everywhere.
items.explanation: Load is used for things carried by a vehicle like a lorry.

items.sentence: The area where the (cargo / load) is stored on a plane can actually get extremely cold...
items.explanation: Cargo is the standard term for goods on a ship or plane.

exercises.label: E
exercises.instruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: The police officer told the homeless man to move ___ and he walked slowly and painfully down the road.
items.explanation: 'Move on' means to leave a place and go elsewhere.

items.sentence: Len has already left on foot, but if we take the car then maybe we can still head him ___ before he gets home.
items.explanation: 'Head sb off' means to intercept someone.

items.sentence: Would you mind moving ___ so that I can sit here?
items.explanation: 'Move over' means to shift position to make space.

items.sentence: A young man was riding his motorbike on the pavement and everyone was forced to step ___ to let him go past.
items.explanation: 'Step aside' means to move out of the way.

items.sentence: The area has been cordoned ___ following a bomb threat.
items.explanation: 'Cordon off' means to seal an area.

items.sentence: As I leant on the table, it tipped ___ and I fell over.
items.explanation: 'Tip up' means one end rises while the other falls.

items.sentence: My mum's a bit upset because my sister's moving ___ to go to university next week.
items.explanation: 'Move out' means to leave one's home permanently.

items.sentence: Daryl managed to keep up with the leader for most of the race, but eventually he started to fall ___ .
items.explanation: 'Fall behind' means to move slower and get left behind.

exercises.label: F
exercises.instruction: Complete using a phrasal verb with a word from box A in the right form and a word from box B.

items.sentence: Dimitri thought he heard a funny noise coming from the car engine, so he ___ to investigate.
items.explanation: To move to the side of the road and stop.

items.sentence: The police ___ the crowd ___ so that the ambulances could get to the scene more quickly.
items.explanation: To prevent someone from moving forward.

items.sentence: The show was so boring that we ___ after half an hour.
items.explanation: To leave a performance/meeting early.

items.sentence: Remind me to ___ on the way to your grandma's to get her a birthday card.
items.explanation: To visit a place briefly during a journey.

items.sentence: I wish you wouldn't ___ on me like that! You frightened me to death!
items.explanation: To approach someone quietly and surprise them.

items.sentence: Martha didn't want to interrupt the party, so she just ___ quietly without anyone noticing.
items.explanation: To leave secretly or quietly.

items.sentence: Are you really thinking of ___ with Josie? What about your own flat?
items.explanation: To start living in a new house with someone.

items.sentence: It seems that the explorers ___ after losing their map and wandered in the jungle for days.
items.explanation: To get lost or go the wrong way.

exercises.label: G
exercises.instruction: The phrasal verb cordon off means something like 'prevent people entering'. Tick the words which could be used to complete this sentence to form phrasal verbs with a similar meaning.

items.sentence: block off
items.explanation: Similar meaning to cordon off.

items.sentence: close off
items.explanation: Similar meaning to cordon off.

items.sentence: line off
items.explanation: Not a common phrasal verb with this meaning.

items.sentence: fence off
items.explanation: Similar meaning, specifically with a fence.

items.sentence: put off
items.explanation: Means to delay.

items.sentence: rope off
items.explanation: Similar meaning, specifically with a rope.

items.sentence: seal off
items.explanation: Similar meaning to cordon off.

items.sentence: take off
items.explanation: Means to leave the ground (plane).

exercises.label: H
exercises.instruction: Complete using the phrases in the box in the correct form. Change sb, sth or do into something appropriate.

items.sentence: Look at the time! We'd better ___ or we're going to be late.
items.explanation: Collocation meaning to hurry up.

items.sentence: My parents say they ___ when they met on holiday in Turkey.
items.explanation: Standard phrase.

items.sentence: Your essay isn't good enough. It's obvious to me that you ___ , rather than taking your time.
items.explanation: To do something quickly and likely carelessly.

items.sentence: I've always wanted to fly and I would ___ to visit the pilot in the cockpit.
items.explanation: To eagerly accept an opportunity.

items.sentence: One of the things I love about this house is that it ___ the river.
items.explanation: To have its back facing something.

items.sentence: I don't want to ___ , but there's a chance we might be flying to America!
items.explanation: To make someone expect something good.

items.sentence: I can't believe you ___ Ben what I said about him.
items.explanation: Phrase 'go and do sth' usually expresses annoyance.

items.sentence: There's no need to ___ me - I wasn't the one who crashed the car!
items.explanation: To attack or criticize someone suddenly.

items.sentence: I've got a lot of homework, so let me ___ first and then I'll call you.
items.answer: get it out of the way
items.explanation: To finish a task so it's no longer a burden.

items.sentence: You travel so much! I can't ___ all the countries you've been to.
items.explanation: To stay informed about something.

items.sentence: Since you're going that way, do you think you could ___ my house?
items.explanation: To let someone out of a vehicle at a certain place.

items.sentence: Those sunglasses were expensive - take them off in the pool or you'll ___ .
items.explanation: Standard phrase.

exercises.label: I
exercises.instruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: In the distance, a steam train ___ slowly into view.
items.explanation: 'Come into view' is a common collocation.

items.sentence: Don't ___ your voice at me! I haven't done anything wrong!
items.explanation: 'Raise your voice' means to shout.

items.sentence: We need to book our ferry tickets ___ some point, so remind me later.
items.explanation: 'At some point' means sometime.

items.sentence: Suddenly, the door flew ___ and there stood Mrs Singh.
items.explanation: 'Fly open' means to open suddenly and with force.

items.sentence: It's the ___ hour, so there'll be a lot of traffic on the roads.
items.explanation: 'Rush hour' is the peak traffic time.

items.sentence: My dad's beginning to ___ grey, and he's only 35.
items.explanation: 'Turn grey' refers to hair color changing.

items.sentence: Even when his flight was cancelled, Jake still managed to ___ a smile.
items.explanation: 'Raise a smile' means to show a smile.

items.sentence: Do you know the ___ to Ed's house from here?
items.explanation: 'Know the way' means to know the route.

items.sentence: Sam's not coming, but lsabelle's ___ the way, so she should be here soon.
items.explanation: 'On the way' means in the process of traveling.

items.sentence: I can't believe that woman just tried to ___ the queue and get to the front first.
items.explanation: 'Jump the queue' means to go ahead of others waiting.

exercises.label: J
exercises.instruction: For each question, write one word which can be used in all three sentences.

items.sentence: I don't understand what ___ Michael to leave his family like that. / The kids almost ___ me crazy when we were on holiday. / I thought the journalist ___ his point home well...
items.explanation: Drove (past of drive) works in 'drive sb crazy', 'drive a point home', and as a motivation.

items.sentence: We decided to ___ towards the small town... / Joshua Reed has been appointed to ___ the committee... / Which places would you say ___ the list of most popular...?
items.explanation: Head (verb) means to go in a direction, lead a committee, or be at the top of a list.

items.sentence: I'm starting to ___ to the conclusion that you don't... / How did you ___ to lose your passport? / I know it's going to ___ as a bit of a shock to you...
items.explanation: Come to a conclusion, come to lose sth, come as a shock.

items.sentence: We need to keep up a ___ pace... / Corey and Angela seem to have a very ___ relationship... / Kelly gave me a ___ look...
items.explanation: Steady pace, steady relationship, steady look.

items.sentence: We always make a ___ of inviting... / Don't mention the fact... - it's a bit of a sore ___ with him. / ...that's beside the ___ ...
items.explanation: Make a point of, a sore point, beside the point.

items.sentence: It's hard to believe that Sandra ___ 50 this year... / The police officer ___ her gun on the man... / I ___ to say something to Connor, but he had already left.
items.explanation: Turned (reached an age), turned a gun on sb, turned to do sth.

items.sentence: Jacob ___ his doctor's advice... / Hannah decided... and her friends soon ___ suit. / I ___ the speaker's argument up to the final point...
items.explanation: Followed advice, followed suit, followed an argument.

items.sentence: My dad's involved in ___ a campaign... / These vehicles have the advantage of ___ on electricity... / You won't be able to avoid ___ into problems...
items.explanation: Running a campaign, running on petrol/electricity, running into problems.

exercises.label: K
exercises.instruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: From our house to the centre of town is about four miles as the ___ flies.
items.explanation: 'As the crow flies' means in a straight line.

items.sentence: I know the brochure said the villa was 'peaceful', but it was actually in the ___ of nowhere.
items.explanation: 'In the middle of nowhere' means very remote.

items.sentence: As soon as we got to the pool, the kids made a ___ for the shop selling ice creams.
items.explanation: 'Make a beeline for' means to go directly towards something.

items.sentence: The soldier shouted 'Halt!' and I stopped dead in my ___ .
items.explanation: 'Stop dead in your tracks' means to stop suddenly.

items.sentence: Cambodia? That's a bit off the beaten ___ , isn't it?
items.explanation: 'Off the beaten track' means in an isolated place.

items.sentence: Take the next left, then first right, and just follow your ___ until you see the stadium on your left.
items.explanation: 'Follow your nose' means to go straight forward.

items.sentence: Wandering the streets of Prague, I lost my ___ , so I had to ask the way to the castle.
items.explanation: 'Lose your bearings' means to become lost or confused about direction.

items.sentence: We were quite lucky because our hotel was only a ___ throw away from the beach.
items.explanation: 'A stone's throw away' means very close.

items.sentence: It's about 20 minutes longer, but if we take the scenic ___ you'll be able to take some photos.
items.explanation: 'Take the scenic route' means to take a more attractive path.

items.sentence: Turn right here and we'll ___ a short cut through the side roads.
items.explanation: 'Take a short cut' means to follow a shorter path.

exercises.label: L
exercises.instruction: Use the word given in capitals at the end of the line to form a word that fits in the space in the same line.

items.sentence: ...provide access to the ___ floors of buildings...
items.explanation: From UP. Refers to higher floors.

items.sentence: ...often, in fact, make them ___ .
items.explanation: From ACCESS. Means impossible to reach.

items.sentence: For many elderly people and others with limited ___ ...
items.explanation: From MOBILE. The ability to move.

items.sentence: ...getting upstairs can be a daily problem to be ___ .
items.explanation: From COME. To succeed in dealing with a problem.

items.sentence: Designs have ___ many changes over the years...
items.explanation: From GO. To experience something.

items.sentence: ...stairlifts have become ___ safer and easier to use.
items.explanation: From PROGRESS. Gradually.

items.sentence: The user controls how ___ the seat moves...
items.explanation: From RAPID. Quickly.

items.sentence: ...as it travels from the bottom of the stairs to the ___ at the top.
items.explanation: From LAND. The flat area at the top of a staircase.

items.sentence: In today's models, the ___ is controlled by computers...
items.explanation: From MOVE. The act of moving.

items.sentence: ...designed to ___ constant use.
items.explanation: From STAND. To be strong enough to remain undamaged.

exercises.label: M
exercises.instruction: Each of the words in bold is in an incorrect form. Rewrite them correctly.

items.sentence: The situation has been getting ___ worse for many years now.
items.explanation: Adverb form required.

items.sentence: The heavy snow meant that the mountain roads were ___ for over a week.
items.explanation: Meaning 'impossible to travel through'.

items.sentence: People are being advised not to travel to the area because of the ___ conflict.
items.explanation: Meaning 'continuing to exist'.

items.sentence: Luckily, Tyrone made a very ___ recovery after the car accident.
items.explanation: Adjective form required.

items.sentence: The service at the hotel was ___ , and I'd definitely recommend it.
items.explanation: Meaning 'excellent'.

items.sentence: Every child needs security and ___ in their life.
items.explanation: Noun form required.

items.sentence: It's not always easy to make the ___ from primary school to secondary school.
items.explanation: Noun form required.

items.sentence: I sat completely ___ as the spider crawled along my arm.
items.explanation: Meaning 'without moving'.

exercises.label: N
exercises.instruction: The prefix de-, as in destabilise, can be added to some verbs, nouns and adjectives in order to give them a negative meaning. Tick the words which can be made negative by adding de-.

items.sentence: caffeinated
items.explanation: Valid with de-.

items.sentence: direct
items.explanation: Uses in-, not de-.

items.sentence: form
items.explanation: Valid with de-.

items.sentence: hydrate
items.explanation: Valid with de-.

items.sentence: motivate
items.explanation: Valid with de-.

items.sentence: regulate
items.explanation: Valid with de-.

items.sentence: satisfy
items.explanation: Uses dis-, not de-.

items.sentence: sensitise
items.explanation: Valid with de-.

items.sentence: understand
items.explanation: Uses mis-, not de-.

items.sentence: value
items.explanation: Valid with de-.

exercises.label: O
exercises.instruction: Complete the sentences using words formed in exercise N.

items.sentence: Drinking alcohol when it's very hot can ___ you.
items.explanation: To lose too much water from the body.

items.sentence: The government should ___ this industry.
items.explanation: To remove government controls.

items.sentence: I have to drink ___ coffee or I can't sleep.
items.explanation: Coffee without caffeine.

items.sentence: If you don't tell staff how well they're doing, it can ___ them.
items.explanation: To make someone less interested in their work.

items.sentence: Don't you think violent films ___ people to violence in real life?
items.explanation: To make someone less sensitive to something.

items.sentence: You'll ___ your feet if you don't get shoes that fit you properly.
items.explanation: To damage the shape of something.

items.sentence: They've decided to ___ the currency, so it'll be worth a lot less.
items.explanation: To reduce the value of a currency.
